requirement.original: Add an IS_SUBAGENT gate on the BLESSED_BRIDGE_RE path in pretool-git-privilege-guard.py so that if a subagent tries to commit with the auto-bulk: prefix, the privilege guard rejects it even if a valid bulk-commit sentinel exists, because only the /commit orchestrator (not a subagent) should legitimately use that code path.
requirement.clarified: In _evaluate_commit (hooks/pretool-git-privilege-guard.py:546-589), add a check before _has_bulk_commit_sentinel: if data.get('agent_id') is truthy, block with exit 2 and a message citing BLOCKED: auto-bulk commit from subagent context.
requirement.what: IS_SUBAGENT check inserted at the start of the BLESSED_BRIDGE_RE branch in _evaluate_commit
requirement.why: Security defense-in-depth: a subagent that knows the auto-bulk: prefix and observes a valid sentinel on disk could currently commit unilaterally; this gate closes that path.

included: hooks/tests/test_bulk_commit_sentinel.py (new test coverage for subagent gate)
excluded: _evaluate_command early allowlist fast path (line 776 — user-authorized bypass; documented only)

success_criteria: Subagent + auto-bulk + valid sentinel => exit 2 + BLOCKED: auto-bulk commit from subagent context in stderr
_has_bulk_commit_sentinel is never called when agent_id is truthy and BLESSED_BRIDGE_RE matches
Main-agent + auto-bulk + valid sentinel + no active grant => exit 0 (no regression)
Subagent + non-auto-bulk + no grant => existing default-deny unchanged
constraints: Use data.get('agent_id') truthiness check (consistent with _has_do_consent at line 132)
New block must precede _has_bulk_commit_sentinel call — ordering is the security property
Do not modify the early allowlist fast path in _evaluate_command

out_of_scope_observations.observation: The /commit --bulk flow currently dispatches changelog-analyst subagent to run git commit -F with auto-bulk: prefix. The new IS_SUBAGENT gate will break this flow. A forward-fix cycle must migrate commit.md + changelog-analyst.md so the /commit orchestrator runs commits directly from main-agent context.

root_cause_analysis.symptom: A subagent that knows the auto-bulk: prefix and observes a valid bulk-commit sentinel on disk can call git commit without being the /commit orchestrator.
root_cause_analysis.root_cause: _evaluate_commit at line 548-561 calls _has_bulk_commit_sentinel(data) without first checking data.get('agent_id'). The sentinel is globally discoverable (global glob fallback at line 501) and multi-use.
root_cause_analysis.root_cause_commit: N/A — design gap, not a regression
root_cause_analysis.why_introduced: The bulk-commit sentinel was added to authorize changelog-analyst (a subagent) to make multiple commits per session. The subagent-exclusion gate was never considered because the design intended changelog-analyst to USE the path.
root_cause_analysis.why_problematic: The user's new policy decision is that only the /commit orchestrator (main-agent) may commit with the auto-bulk: prefix. The sentinel alone is insufficient because any subagent can find it.
root_cause_analysis.timeline: Design gap since bulk-commit sentinel was introduced (task pre-2026-05-26).
observations: _evaluate_commit line 548: 'if msg and BLESSED_BRIDGE_RE.search(msg):' — no agent_id check precedes _has_bulk_commit_sentinel call (inferred: source read lines 546-562)
_has_do_consent line 132: 'if data.get("agent_id"): return False' — pattern to replicate (inferred: source read line 132)
_check_git_allowlist line 160: 'if data.get("agent_id"): return False' — same pattern for legacy path (inferred: source read line 160)
root_cause_analysis.diagnosis_completeness: incomplete (inferred only — no runtime hook invocation performed; hook is a PreToolUse guard so live testing requires a mock payload)
root_cause_analysis.localization_evidence: Source read of hooks/pretool-git-privilege-guard.py lines 546-562 shows _has_bulk_commit_sentinel called without prior agent_id check.

requirements_decomposition.source_phrase: if a subagent tries to commit with the auto-bulk: prefix, the privilege guard should reject it even if a valid bulk-commit sentinel exists
requirements_decomposition.acceptance_criterion: AC1: subagent + auto-bulk + valid sentinel => exit 2 + BLOCKED: auto-bulk commit from subagent context

requirements_decomposition.source_phrase: only the /commit orchestrator (not a subagent) should legitimately use that code path
requirements_decomposition.acceptance_criterion: AC2: main-agent + auto-bulk + valid sentinel + no active single-use grant => exit 0 (existing behavior preserved)

component_chain.role: main() entry point — reads stdin JSON, calls _evaluate_command
_evaluate_command — dispatches to _evaluate_commit for git commit commands

pre_existing_guards.code_snippet:
if data.get('agent_id'):  # subagents cannot use /do
        return False
pre_existing_guards.purpose: Prevents subagents from using /do consent flag
Prevents subagents from using legacy /allow grants for git operations
Requires valid bulk-commit sentinel before allowing auto-bulk commits

setup.reason: non-UI -- hook; modifies hooks/pretool-git-privilege-guard.py only. Does not produce (1) rendered UI changes, (2) browser interaction, (3) Playwright invocation, (4) screenshot evidence, or (5) any change to user-triggered UI code paths.

evidence.observed: The M5 allowlist permits source venv/bin/activate && python3 /root/.claude/scripts/write-bulk-commit-sentinel.py for ALL callers including subagents. An IS_SUBAGENT gate on the BLESSED_BRIDGE_RE path would add a second layer of defense.

measured.value: _evaluate_commit calls _has_bulk_commit_sentinel(data) at line 551 without any prior check of data.get('agent_id')

expected.value: data.get('agent_id') truthy => _block(...) with exit 2 before _has_bulk_commit_sentinel is called

evidence.gap: One missing check: if data.get('agent_id'): _block('BLOCKED: auto-bulk commit from subagent context...')

reference_source.description: Source code read in this session — _has_do_consent pattern at line 132 uses data.get('agent_id') truthiness as the definitive subagent detection idiom

novelty_check.rationale: No prior attempts — first cycle for this specific fix.

context.codebase_state: master branch, working tree has untracked inspector reports
context.recent_commits: c88eed8 auto-bulk: end-of-cycle commit for master: docs(dev)

development_approach.strategy: Insert one 3-line guard block at hooks/pretool-git-privilege-guard.py line 549 (inside BLESSED_BRIDGE_RE branch, before _has_bulk_commit_sentinel call). Update test file.
development_approach.validation_approach: Run hooks/tests/test_bulk_commit_sentinel.py with pytest; verify new test cases AC1/AC2/AC3 pass.

_risk_level_doc: medium: modifies a security-critical hook but the change is a single guard block. The changelog-analyst breakage is a known/intended side effect documented in out_of_scope_observations.

codex_consult.feedback_summary: Codex produced 6 findings. Findings 1-5 had PROPOSED_FIX; finding 6 was OBSERVATION_ONLY.

findings.issue: AC coverage should include integration path through _evaluate_command/_main, not only _evaluate_commit directly — early allowlist fast-path at line 776 can return before _evaluate_commit runs.
findings.proposed_fix: Either move/exclude commit handling before that fast-path, or explicitly document /allow as out of scope for the subagent-auto-bulk threat model.
findings.rationale: Added to Non-Goals with explicit documentation: user-authorized /allow grants that match auto-bulk commits are intentional bypasses. No structural change to _evaluate_command needed; the fast-path only fires when a sentinel-grant matches, which requires explicit /allow by the user.

findings.issue: AC2 was missing the active-grant exception: current behavior defers auto-bulk when _has_active_commit_grant() is true.
findings.proposed_fix: Rewrite AC2 to explicitly state: main-agent + auto-bulk + valid sentinel + no active single-use commit grant => allow.
findings.rationale: AC2 updated to include 'AND no active single-use commit grant (_has_active_commit_grant() returns False)' precondition.

findings.issue: AC3 was ambiguous — 'non-auto-bulk commit' is not always default-denied if a /commit grant or /allow grant exists.
findings.proposed_fix: Rewrite as: subagent + non-auto-bulk + no matching commit/allow grant => existing default-deny unchanged.
findings.rationale: AC3 updated to specify 'AND no matching /commit grant or /allow sentinel-grant exists'.

findings.issue: Subagent detection semantics: 'non-null' vs truthiness — agent_id: '' is falsy but non-null.
findings.proposed_fix: Use bool(data.get('agent_id')) or the same data.get('agent_id') truthiness as _has_do_consent:132, unless empty string is contractually impossible.
findings.rationale: Spec now explicitly says 'use data.get('agent_id') truthiness check' consistent with line 132, and documents empty string = not-a-subagent intentionally.

findings.issue: Need a test proving the new block happens BEFORE _has_bulk_commit_sentinel, e.g. patch _has_bulk_commit_sentinel to raise if called when agent_id present.
findings.proposed_fix: Add ordering-proof assertion to AC1 test.
findings.rationale: AC1 now includes: 'AND _has_bulk_commit_sentinel is NOT called (ordering proof: patch it to raise; test passes because the subagent block fires first)'.

findings.issue: The requirement reverses the documented /commit --bulk design: commit.md and write-bulk-commit-sentinel.py currently say the sentinel authorizes changelog-analyst (a subagent) to commit. Hook-only fix will intentionally break that flow.
findings.rationale: User requirement explicitly states this breakage is intended. Logged in out_of_scope_observations as a forward-fix cycle prerequisite. Not blocking this spec.

codex_consult.feedback_incorporated: AC2 and AC3 rewritten with precise preconditions; AC1 ordering-proof test assertion added; agent_id semantics clarified to use existing truthiness pattern; /allow fast-path documented as user-authorized bypass (no structural change needed); changelog-analyst breakage logged in out_of_scope_observations.

research_findings.reason: Straightforward single-file guard insertion following existing patterns in the same file.
